Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. 1	# Plan d’implémentation — Usage history snapshots
2	
3	## Résumé
4	
5	Ajouter une capture d’historique append-only des métriques d’usage, indépendante du `UsagePoller`, via un nouvel acteur `SnapshotRecorder` déclenché toutes les minutes pendant que l’app tourne. Le recorder lit l’état courant depuis `UsagesFileManager`, transforme chaque métrique en `SnapshotEntry`, écrit dans un fichier JSONL journalier sous `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/usage-history/{year}/{month}/{year}-{month}-{day}.jsonl`, et saute silencieusement les ticks sans données ou sans changement depuis le dernier snapshot écrit.
6	
7	## Changements d’implémentation
8	
9	- Ajouter un modèle persistant `SnapshotEntry` dans la lib, `Codable`/`Equatable`/`Sendable`, avec les champs:
10	  - `timestamp`
11	  - `vendor`
12	  - `account`
13	  - `metricName`
14	  - `kind`
15	  - pour `time-window`: `usagePercent`
16	  - pour `pay-as-you-go`: `currentAmount`, `currency`
17	- Garder un shape plat par ligne JSONL, une ligne par métrique, sans réutiliser le schema `usages.json`.
18	
19	- Ajouter un protocole injectable de type `SnapshotRecording` et une implémentation acteur `SnapshotRecorder`.
20	  - Dépendances: chemin racine d’historique, logger, `FileManager`, horloge/date-provider testable.
21	  - API recommandée: `recordSnapshot(from file: UsagesFile, now: Date = Date()) async`.
22	  - Responsabilités:
23	    - filtrer les `VendorUsageEntry` sans métriques
24	    - aplatir les métriques vers `[SnapshotEntry]`
25	    - ne rien écrire si la liste est vide
26	    - comparer avec le dernier snapshot effectivement écrit et ne rien écrire si toutes les lignes seraient identiques hors timestamp
27	    - créer automatiquement les dossiers année/mois
28	    - ouvrir le fichier du jour en append, sans écraser l’existant
29	    - basculer automatiquement vers un nouveau fichier au changement de jour
30	  - La déduplication doit être faite sur le contenu métier d’un tick complet, pas ligne par ligne isolée, pour éviter des fichiers partiellement dupliqués.
31	
32	- Ajouter un cadenceur dédié, séparé de `UsagePoller`.
33	  - Recommandation: un nouvel acteur léger `SnapshotScheduler` qui possède une `Task<Void, Never>?`, dort 60 s entre deux ticks et appelle `UsagesFileManager.read()` puis `SnapshotRecorder.recordSnapshot(...)`.
34	  - Le scheduler ne dépend pas du fetch interval utilisateur et reste fixé à 1 minute.
35	  - Il doit être démarré et stoppé depuis `AppDelegate` au même niveau que `UsagePoller` et les account monitors.
36	  - Ne pas brancher cette feature sur `UsageStore` ni sur la sortie directe du `UsagePoller`.
37	
38	- Ajouter une petite abstraction de temps/date réutilisable pour les tests du recorder/scheduler.
39	  - Si possible, réutiliser le pattern `ClockProvider` déjà présent dans `UsageStore`.
40	  - Le découpage journalier doit utiliser le calendrier local explicite de l’implémentation, de façon déterministe en test.
41	
42	- Logging attendu:
43	  - `debug` pour tick sans données ou sans changement
44	  - `info` pour écriture réussie avec nombre de lignes append
45	  - `warning`/`error` si création de dossier, ouverture de fichier, encodage ou append échoue
46	  - Une erreur d’écriture ne doit pas casser la boucle du scheduler.
47	
48	## Interfaces / contrats publics
49	
50	- Nouveau protocole lib:
51	  - `SnapshotRecording`
52	- Nouveau type lib:
53	  - `SnapshotEntry`
54	- Nouveau type lib ou interne selon préférence d’exposition:
55	  - `SnapshotRecorder`
56	  - `SnapshotScheduler`
57	- Aucun changement au format de `usages.json`.
58	- Aucun changement UI.
59	- Aucun réglage utilisateur exposé pour l’intervalle ou la rétention dans cette epic.
60	
61	## Tests et scénarios
62	
63	- `SnapshotRecorderTests`
64	  - écrit un snapshot nominal avec plusieurs vendors/comptes/métriques
65	  - ne crée aucun fichier si `UsagesFile` ne contient aucune métrique
66	  - crée le fichier au premier tick
67	  - append sans écraser après restart simulé
68	  - skip quand le tick courant est identique au dernier snapshot écrit
69	  - écrit à nouveau dès qu’une seule valeur de métrique change
70	  - rollover minuit: écrit la veille puis le lendemain dans deux fichiers distincts sans perdre la dernière ligne du jour précédent
71	  - encode chaque ligne en JSON valide décodable en `SnapshotEntry`
72	  - ignore proprement les métriques `.unknown(...)`
73	
74	- `SnapshotSchedulerTests`
75	  - `start()` idempotent
76	  - `stop()` idempotent
77	  - un tick lit bien `UsagesFileManager` puis appelle le recorder
78	  - une erreur du recorder n’interrompt pas les ticks suivants
79	
80	- Test d’intégration léger via `AppDelegate` ou test de wiring équivalent
81	  - le scheduler est bien instancié et démarré au lancement
82	  - il est bien stoppé à la fermeture
83	
84	## Hypothèses et choix figés
85	
86	- Déduplication retenue: `skip unchanged`.
87	  - Cela suit la scope et les acceptance criteria actuelles; la note finale de la roadmap devra être considérée obsolète ou corrigée.
88	- Source de vérité retenue: `UsagesFileManager.read()`.
89	  - Cela garde la feature dans la couche lib, hors UI, et reflète exactement l’état persistant partagé.
90	- Les entrées avec `lastError` mais des métriques encore présentes dans `usages.json` sont snapshotées.
91	  - Le contrat de la feature porte sur les métriques disponibles, pas sur le succès du dernier fetch.
92	- Le scheduler démarre au lancement de l’app, puis attend 60 s entre ticks; pas de tick immédiat imposé par cette epic.
93	- La rétention/rotation des fichiers d’historique reste hors scope.
94	- Le chemin de stockage est strictement celui de la roadmap: `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/usage-history/{year}/{month}/{year}-{month}-{day}.jsonl`.
95

# Plan d’implémentation — Usage history snapshots

## Résumé

Ajouter une capture d’historique append-only des métriques d’usage, indépendante du `UsagePoller`, via un nouvel acteur `SnapshotRecorder` déclenché toutes les minutes pendant que l’app tourne. Le recorder lit l’état courant depuis `UsagesFileManager`, transforme chaque métrique en `SnapshotEntry`, écrit dans un fichier JSONL journalier sous `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/usage-history/{year}/{month}/{year}-{month}-{day}.jsonl`, et saute silencieusement les ticks sans données ou sans changement depuis le dernier snapshot écrit.

## Changements d’implémentation

- Ajouter un modèle persistant `SnapshotEntry` dans la lib, `Codable`/`Equatable`/`Sendable`, avec les champs:
  - `timestamp`
  - `vendor`
  - `account`
  - `metricName`
  - `kind`
  - pour `time-window`: `usagePercent`
  - pour `pay-as-you-go`: `currentAmount`, `currency`
- Garder un shape plat par ligne JSONL, une ligne par métrique, sans réutiliser le schema `usages.json`.

- Ajouter un protocole injectable de type `SnapshotRecording` et une implémentation acteur `SnapshotRecorder`.
  - Dépendances: chemin racine d’historique, logger, `FileManager`, horloge/date-provider testable.
  - API recommandée: `recordSnapshot(from file: UsagesFile, now: Date = Date()) async`.
  - Responsabilités:
    - filtrer les `VendorUsageEntry` sans métriques
    - aplatir les métriques vers `[SnapshotEntry]`
    - ne rien écrire si la liste est vide
    - comparer avec le dernier snapshot effectivement écrit et ne rien écrire si toutes les lignes seraient identiques hors timestamp
    - créer automatiquement les dossiers année/mois
    - ouvrir le fichier du jour en append, sans écraser l’existant
    - basculer automatiquement vers un nouveau fichier au changement de jour
  - La déduplication doit être faite sur le contenu métier d’un tick complet, pas ligne par ligne isolée, pour éviter des fichiers partiellement dupliqués.

- Ajouter un cadenceur dédié, séparé de `UsagePoller`.
  - Recommandation: un nouvel acteur léger `SnapshotScheduler` qui possède une `Task<Void, Never>?`, dort 60 s entre deux ticks et appelle `UsagesFileManager.read()` puis `SnapshotRecorder.recordSnapshot(...)`.
  - Le scheduler ne dépend pas du fetch interval utilisateur et reste fixé à 1 minute.
  - Il doit être démarré et stoppé depuis `AppDelegate` au même niveau que `UsagePoller` et les account monitors.
  - Ne pas brancher cette feature sur `UsageStore` ni sur la sortie directe du `UsagePoller`.

- Ajouter une petite abstraction de temps/date réutilisable pour les tests du recorder/scheduler.
  - Si possible, réutiliser le pattern `ClockProvider` déjà présent dans `UsageStore`.
  - Le découpage journalier doit utiliser le calendrier local explicite de l’implémentation, de façon déterministe en test.

- Logging attendu:
  - `debug` pour tick sans données ou sans changement
  - `info` pour écriture réussie avec nombre de lignes append
  - `warning`/`error` si création de dossier, ouverture de fichier, encodage ou append échoue
  - Une erreur d’écriture ne doit pas casser la boucle du scheduler.

## Interfaces / contrats publics

- Nouveau protocole lib:
  - `SnapshotRecording`
- Nouveau type lib:
  - `SnapshotEntry`
- Nouveau type lib ou interne selon préférence d’exposition:
  - `SnapshotRecorder`
  - `SnapshotScheduler`
- Aucun changement au format de `usages.json`.
- Aucun changement UI.
- Aucun réglage utilisateur exposé pour l’intervalle ou la rétention dans cette epic.

## Tests et scénarios

- `SnapshotRecorderTests`
  - écrit un snapshot nominal avec plusieurs vendors/comptes/métriques
  - ne crée aucun fichier si `UsagesFile` ne contient aucune métrique
  - crée le fichier au premier tick
  - append sans écraser après restart simulé
  - skip quand le tick courant est identique au dernier snapshot écrit
  - écrit à nouveau dès qu’une seule valeur de métrique change
  - rollover minuit: écrit la veille puis le lendemain dans deux fichiers distincts sans perdre la dernière ligne du jour précédent
  - encode chaque ligne en JSON valide décodable en `SnapshotEntry`
  - ignore proprement les métriques `.unknown(...)`

- `SnapshotSchedulerTests`
  - `start()` idempotent
  - `stop()` idempotent
  - un tick lit bien `UsagesFileManager` puis appelle le recorder
  - une erreur du recorder n’interrompt pas les ticks suivants

- Test d’intégration léger via `AppDelegate` ou test de wiring équivalent
  - le scheduler est bien instancié et démarré au lancement
  - il est bien stoppé à la fermeture

## Hypothèses et choix figés

- Déduplication retenue: `skip unchanged`.
  - Cela suit la scope et les acceptance criteria actuelles; la note finale de la roadmap devra être considérée obsolète ou corrigée.
- Source de vérité retenue: `UsagesFileManager.read()`.
  - Cela garde la feature dans la couche lib, hors UI, et reflète exactement l’état persistant partagé.
- Les entrées avec `lastError` mais des métriques encore présentes dans `usages.json` sont snapshotées.
  - Le contrat de la feature porte sur les métriques disponibles, pas sur le succès du dernier fetch.
- Le scheduler démarre au lancement de l’app, puis attend 60 s entre ticks; pas de tick immédiat imposé par cette epic.
- La rétention/rotation des fichiers d’historique reste hors scope.
- Le chemin de stockage est strictement celui de la roadmap: `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/usage-history/{year}/{month}/{year}-{month}-{day}.jsonl`.

# Markdown authoring rules

## Language: English only

All Markdown files in this repository MUST be written in English, regardless of the conversation language. This includes `CLAUDE.md`, every file under `docs/`, `README.md`, and any new `*.md`.

- When creating a new Markdown file, write it in English from the start even if the user's prompt is in another language.
- When editing an existing Markdown file that contains non-English prose, translate the edited sections to English instead of preserving mixed languages.
- Code blocks, file paths, commands, and identifiers keep their original form — this rule applies to natural-language prose only.

## Write for longevity

Markdown in this repository is long-lived context. Optimize each file so it does not need to be rewritten as the code evolves. Avoid anything that ties documentation to a specific moment in time or to renamable artifacts:

- Do NOT reference specific commit SHAs, PR numbers, or branch names.
- Do NOT describe the project as being in a transient state ("currently a Hello World", "not yet implemented", "WIP"). If a feature does not exist, simply do not document it.
- Do NOT speculate about intent or future direction ("the name suggests…", "will eventually…"). Document what the code actually does today in terms that remain true tomorrow.
- Do NOT list every file, symbol, or dependency when a structural description is enough — exhaustive lists drift out of date quickly.
- Avoid naming specific files, types, or symbols that could be renamed or moved. Prefer describing roles and responsibilities ("the SwiftUI entry point", "the package manifest") over fixed identifiers. When a path is truly stable because a tool mandates it (e.g. `Package.swift` for Swift Package Manager, `.claude/rules/` for Claude Code), it is fine to use it.
- Do NOT duplicate information that already lives authoritatively in code or tool manifests (version numbers, platform minimums, target lists). Point readers at the source of truth instead of mirroring it.

When in doubt, ask: "Will this sentence still be accurate after the next three features ship, or after a plausible rename?" If not, rephrase or remove it.

## Single source of truth across Markdown files

When two Markdown files cover the same topic, exactly one is the authoritative spec; the other references it and adds only what is genuinely its own (workflow steps, scaffolding procedure, agent phases, etc.).

This applies in particular to these pairs:

- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (skill that implements it)
- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/agents/<name>.md` (agent prompt that uses it)
- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/commands/<name>.md` (slash command that triggers it)

Rules:

- Templates, file formats, naming conventions, and directory layouts belong in the spec, NOT in the operational artifact.
- The operational artifact MUST start with a one-line pointer ("Read `docs/<topic>.md` first; it defines X, Y, Z.") and MUST include a conflict-resolution line ("If this artifact disagrees with `docs/<topic>.md`, the spec wins.").
- Before adding concrete content (a template block, a format string, a list of sections) to an operational artifact, check whether that content already exists in a `docs/` spec. If it does, reference it instead of copying it.

## Exception: `roadmap/`

Files under `roadmap/` intentionally describe planned, not-yet-implemented work. The "Write for longevity" rules above (no transient state, no speculation about future direction) do NOT apply there — a roadmap is precisely a record of intent and ordering that will evolve as features ship. The English-only rule still applies. See `docs/ROADMAP.md` for the roadmap file conventions.

content.content:
1	# Usage history snapshots
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Periodically record a snapshot of every metric value for every vendor account to a JSONL file, enabling future graph views that show consumption trends over time.
6	
7	## Dependencies
8	
9	None.
10	
11	## Scope
12	
13	- A `SnapshotRecorder` actor that appends one JSON line per metric per tick to a daily JSONL file.
14	- History files are partitioned by calendar day: `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/usage-history/{year}/{month}/{year}-{month}-{day}.jsonl` (e.g. `2025/04/2025-04-19.jsonl`). A new file is created automatically when the day rolls over.
15	- Each line encodes: ISO timestamp, vendor, account, metric name, metric kind, and the kind-specific value (`usagePercent` for `time-window`; `currentAmount` + `currency` for `pay-as-you-go`).
16	- A background timer fires every minute while the app is running.
17	- The timer reads the current state from `UsagesFileManager` (or a shared `UsageStore` if one is introduced).
18	- Snapshots are only written when at least one entry with metrics exists; ticks where no data is available are silently skipped.
19	- A line is appended only when at least one metric value has changed since the last written snapshot; ticks that would produce an identical record to the previous one are silently skipped (avoids bloating the file during idle periods).
20	- Each daily file is opened in append mode; existing history is never overwritten.
21	- `SnapshotRecorder` is injectable via protocol for testing.
22	
23	**Out of scope**
24	
25	- Any UI to display the history data (separate epic).
26	- Pruning or rotating the snapshot file (add to log-cleanup or a dedicated retention epic).
27	- Making the snapshot interval configurable from the UI (can be added once the Settings window epic ships).
28	- Exporting or sharing the history file.
29

# Usage history snapshots

## Goal

Periodically record a snapshot of every metric value for every vendor account to a JSONL file, enabling future graph views that show consumption trends over time.

## Dependencies

None.

## Scope

- A `SnapshotRecorder` actor that appends one JSON line per metric per tick to a daily JSONL file.
- History files are partitioned by calendar day: `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/usage-history/{year}/{month}/{year}-{month}-{day}.jsonl` (e.g. `2025/04/2025-04-19.jsonl`). A new file is created automatically when the day rolls over.
- Each line encodes: ISO timestamp, vendor, account, metric name, metric kind, and the kind-specific value (`usagePercent` for `time-window`; `currentAmount` + `currency` for `pay-as-you-go`).
- A background timer fires every minute while the app is running.
- The timer reads the current state from `UsagesFileManager` (or a shared `UsageStore` if one is introduced).
- Snapshots are only written when at least one entry with metrics exists; ticks where no data is available are silently skipped.
- A line is appended only when at least one metric value has changed since the last written snapshot; ticks that would produce an identical record to the previous one are silently skipped (avoids bloating the file during idle periods).
- Each daily file is opened in append mode; existing history is never overwritten.
- `SnapshotRecorder` is injectable via protocol for testing.

**Out of scope**

- Any UI to display the history data (separate epic).
- Pruning or rotating the snapshot file (add to log-cleanup or a dedicated retention epic).
- Making the snapshot interval configurable from the UI (can be added once the Settings window epic ships).
- Exporting or sharing the history file.

## Acceptance criteria

- After one minute of uptime, today's daily file (`usage-history/{year}/{month}/{year}-{month}-{day}.jsonl`) exists and contains at least one line per active metric.
- Each line is valid JSON parseable as a `SnapshotEntry` struct (timestamp, vendor, account, metricName, kind, value fields).
- Restarting the app appends new lines rather than overwriting existing ones.
- When no data has been fetched yet, no empty or partial lines are written.
- `SnapshotRecorder` has unit tests covering: normal append, skip-when-empty, skip-when-unchanged, file-creation-on-first-tick, and day-rollover (new file created at midnight without losing the last line of the previous day).

## Notes

The snapshot cadence (1 min) is intentionally decoupled from the `UsagePoller` fetch cadence (3 min default). Multiple consecutive snapshot lines may therefore reflect the same underlying metric value between fetches — that is expected and useful for showing "still at X%" on a timeline.

1	import Foundation
2	import Observation
3	
4	// MARK: - Clock abstraction (testable time source)
5	
6	/// Abstracts `Date.now` so tests can inject a fixed point in time.
7	public protocol ClockProvider: Sendable {
8	    func now() -> Date
9	}
10	
11	public struct SystemClock: ClockProvider {
12	    public init() {}
13	    public func now() -> Date { Date() }
14	}
15	
16	// MARK: - Store
17	
18	/// One segment of the menu bar label — a single metric's formatted text, its tier,
19	/// and whether the leading colored dot should be drawn. Pay-as-you-go segments
20	/// render without a dot; time-window segments respect the user's `showDot` toggle.
21	public struct MenuBarSegment: Sendable, Equatable {
22	    public let text: String
23	    public let tier: ConsumptionTier?
24	    public let showDot: Bool
25	
26	    public init(text: String, tier: ConsumptionTier?, showDot: Bool) {
27	        self.text = text
28	        self.tier = tier
29	        self.showDot = showDot
30	    }
31	}
32	
33	/// Formats the user-configured menu bar segments. Iterates the segments declared
34	/// in `AppPreferences.menuBarSegments`, resolves each against the latest usages
35	/// file contents, and exposes the rendered label text + per-segment payloads.
36	/// Falls back to `"--"` when no segment can be rendered.
37	@Observable
38	@MainActor
39	public final class UsageStore {
40	    public private(set) var menuBarText: String = "--"
41	    public private(set) var menuBarTier: ConsumptionTier?
42	    public private(set) var menuBarSegments: [MenuBarSegment] = []
43	    /// Incremented each time handleNewData is called, whether the data is valid or malformed.
44	    /// Tests use this as a reliable signal that the store has consumed the latest yielded item.
45	    public private(set) var dataProcessedCount: Int = 0
46	    public private(set) var entries: [VendorUsageEntry] = []
47	    public private(set) var outagesByVendor: [Vendor: [Outage]] = [:]
48	
49	    // MARK: Dependencies
50	
51	    private let fileWatcher: FileWatching
52	    private let clock: ClockProvider
53	    private let logger: FileLogger
54	    private let preferences: any AppPreferences
55	    private let countdownRefreshSeconds: UInt64
56	
57	    public static let defaultCountdownRefreshSeconds: UInt64 = 60
58	    private static let fallbackText = "--"
59	    private static let rawPreviewMaxBytes = 200
60	
61	    private static func rawPreview(_ data: Data) -> String {
62	        let slice = data.prefix(rawPreviewMaxBytes)
63	        if let text = String(data: slice, encoding: .utf8) {
64	            return "utf8:\(text.debugDescription)"
65	        }
66	        let hex = slice.map { String(format: "%02x", $0) }.joined()
67	        return "hex:\(hex)"
68	    }
69	
70	    // Latest decoded file kept for countdown refresh without re-reading disk
71	    private var lastFile: UsagesFile?
72	
73	    private var watchTask: Task<Void, Never>?
74	    private var countdownTask: Task<Void, Never>?
75	
76	    public init(
77	        fileWatcher: FileWatching,
78	        clock: ClockProvider = SystemClock(),
79	        logger: FileLogger = Loggers.app,
80	        preferences: (any AppPreferences)? = nil,
81	        countdownRefreshSeconds: UInt64 = UsageStore.defaultCountdownRefreshSeconds
82	    ) {
83	        self.fileWatcher = fileWatcher
84	        self.clock = clock
85	        self.logger = logger
86	        // Default preferences mirror the pre-settings-window behaviour (S + W for
87	        // currently-active Claude account) so existing callers and tests that
88	        // don't supply a preferences store still get meaningful output.
89	        self.preferences = preferences ?? Self.makeDefaultPreferences()
90	        self.countdownRefreshSeconds = countdownRefreshSeconds
91	    }
92	
93	    private static func makeDefaultPreferences() -> any AppPreferences {
94	        InMemoryAppPreferences(
95	            menuBarSegments: MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.defaultSegments(),
96	            menuBarSegmentsInitialized: true
97	        )
98	    }
99	
100	    // MARK: Lifecycle
101	
102	    /// Start watching the file and refreshing the countdown.
103	    /// Idempotent — calling again is a no-op.
104	    public func start() {
105	        guard watchTask == nil else { return }
106	
107	        let watcher = fileWatcher
108	        // Self is captured strongly; stop() cancels both tasks to break the cycle
109	        watchTask = Task { [self] in
110	            for await data in watcher.changes() {
111	                guard !Task.isCancelled else { break }
112	                self.handleNewData(data)
113	            }
114	        }
115	
116	        countdownTask = Task { [self] in
117	            let refreshNanos = self.countdownRefreshSeconds * 1_000_000_000
118	            while !Task.isCancelled {
119	                // CancellationError swallowed intentionally; checked on next loop iteration
120	                try? await Task.sleep(nanoseconds: refreshNanos)
121	                guard !Task.isCancelled else { break }
122	                self.refreshMenuBarText()
123	            }
124	        }
125	
126	        trackPreferencesChanges()
127	    }
128	
129	    /// Stop all background tasks. Idempotent.
130	    public func stop() {
131	        watchTask?.cancel()
132	        watchTask = nil
133	        countdownTask?.cancel()
134	        countdownTask = nil
135	    }
136	
137	    // Defensive: cancels tasks when the store is released without an explicit stop() call.
138	    @MainActor deinit { stop() }
139	
140	    // MARK: - Processing
141	
142	    private func handleNewData(_ data: Data) {
143	        dataProcessedCount += 1
144	        do {
145	            let file = try JSONDecoder().decode(UsagesFile.self, from: data)
146	            lastFile = file
147	            entries = file.usages
148	            outagesByVendor = file.outagesByVendor
149	            applyFormat()
150	        } catch {
151	            let preview = Self.rawPreview(data)
152	            logger.log(.error, "UsageStore: JSON decode failed: \(error) — bytes=\(data.count), preview=\(preview)")
153	            lastFile = nil
154	            entries = []
155	            outagesByVendor = [:]
156	            menuBarText = Self.fallbackText
157	            menuBarTier = nil
158	            menuBarSegments = []
159	        }
160	    }
161	
162	    private func refreshMenuBarText() {
163	        guard lastFile != nil else { return }
164	        applyFormat()
165	    }
166	
167	    /// Re-runs formatting whenever `preferences.menuBarSegments` changes so the
168	    /// menu bar updates as the user edits the segment configuration. Tracking
169	    /// fires once per registration — re-arm inside the callback.
170	    private func trackPreferencesChanges() {
171	        withObservationTracking {
172	            _ = preferences.menuBarSegments
173	        } onChange: { [weak self] in
174	            Task { @MainActor in
175	                guard let self else { return }
176	                self.refreshMenuBarText()
177	                self.trackPreferencesChanges()
178	            }
179	        }
180	    }
181	
182	    // MARK: - Formatting
183	
184	    private func applyFormat() {
185	        let now = clock.now()
186	        let configs = preferences.menuBarSegments
187	        var rendered: [MenuBarSegment] = []
188	        for config in configs {
189	            let resolution = MenuBarSegmentResolver.resolve(config: config, entries: entries, now: now)
190	            if let segment = resolution.rendered {
191	                rendered.append(segment)
192	            } else if let issue = resolution.issue {
193	                logger.log(.debug, "UsageStore: skipping segment \(config.metricName) — \(issue)")
194	            }
195	        }
196	        menuBarSegments = rendered
197	        menuBarText = rendered.isEmpty ? Self.fallbackText : rendered.map(\.text).joined(separator: " | ")
198	        menuBarTier = rendered.compactMap(\.tier).max()
199	    }
200	}

1	import Foundation
2	
3	// MARK: - Root
4	
5	/// The top-level file model: `{ usages: [...], outages?: [...] }`.
6	///
7	/// `outages` is an optional sibling written by an upstream status-fetching process.
8	/// When no incident is ongoing the upstream writer is expected to drop the key
9	/// entirely; the encoder here honours that convention by emitting nothing when
10	/// the in-memory array is empty.
11	public struct UsagesFile: Codable, Equatable, Sendable {
12	    public var usages: [VendorUsageEntry]
13	    public var outages: [Outage]
14	
15	    public init(usages: [VendorUsageEntry] = [], outages: [Outage] = []) {
16	        self.usages = usages
17	        self.outages = outages
18	    }
19	
20	    /// Grouped view for the UI — vendors without outages are omitted.
21	    public var outagesByVendor: [Vendor: [Outage]] {
22	        Dictionary(grouping: outages, by: \.vendor)
23	    }
24	
25	    private enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
26	        case usages, outages
27	    }
28	
29	    public init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
30	        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
31	        usages = try container.decodeIfPresent([VendorUsageEntry].self, forKey: .usages) ?? []
32	        outages = try container.decodeIfPresent([Outage].self, forKey: .outages) ?? []
33	    }
34	
35	    public func encode(to encoder: Encoder) throws {
36	        var container = encoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
37	        try container.encode(usages, forKey: .usages)
38	        // Upstream convention: absent key when no ongoing outage — mirror that on write
39	        // so the file never carries a stale empty array between incidents.
40	        if !outages.isEmpty {
41	            try container.encode(outages, forKey: .outages)
42	        }
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	// MARK: - VendorUsageEntry
47	
48	public struct VendorUsageEntry: Codable, Equatable, Sendable, Identifiable {
49	    public var id: String { "\(vendor.rawValue):\(account.rawValue)" }
50	
51	    public let vendor: Vendor
52	    public let account: AccountEmail
53	    public var isActive: Bool
54	    public var lastAcquiredOn: ISODate?
55	    public var lastError: UsageError?
56	    public var metrics: [UsageMetric]
57	
58	    public init(
59	        vendor: Vendor,
60	        account: AccountEmail,
61	        isActive: Bool = false,
62	        lastAcquiredOn: ISODate? = nil,
63	        lastError: UsageError? = nil,
64	        metrics: [UsageMetric] = []
65	    ) {
66	        self.vendor = vendor
67	        self.account = account
68	        self.isActive = isActive
69	        self.lastAcquiredOn = lastAcquiredOn
70	        self.lastError = lastError
71	        self.metrics = metrics
72	    }
73	}
74	
75	// MARK: - Error
76	
77	public struct UsageError: Codable, Equatable, Sendable {
78	    public let timestamp: ISODate
79	    public let type: String
80	
81	    public init(timestamp: ISODate, type: String) {
82	        self.timestamp = timestamp
83	        self.type = type
84	    }
85	}
86	
87	// MARK: - Outage
88	
89	/// An active incident on a vendor's platform, written by an upstream status-fetching process.
90	/// The upstream writer removes entries as incidents resolve, so a missing or empty
91	/// `outages` array means "all clear".
92	public struct Outage: Codable, Equatable, Sendable, Identifiable {
93	    public let vendor: Vendor
94	    public let errorMessage: String
95	    public let severity: OutageSeverity
96	    public let since: ISODate
97	    public let href: URL?
98	
99	    /// Composite id — outages have no stable upstream id in this shape, so we
100	    /// derive one from the fields that together identify a single incident row.
101	    public var id: String {
102	        "\(vendor.rawValue)|\(since.rawValue)|\(href?.absoluteString ?? "")"
103	    }
104	
105	    public init(
106	        vendor: Vendor,
107	        errorMessage: String,
108	        severity: OutageSeverity,
109	        since: ISODate,
110	        href: URL? = nil
111	    ) {
112	        self.vendor = vendor
113	        self.errorMessage = errorMessage
114	        self.severity = severity
115	        self.since = since
116	        self.href = href
117	    }
118	
119	    private enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
120	        case vendor, errorMessage, severity, since, href
121	    }
122	
123	    public init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
124	        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
125	        vendor = try container.decode(Vendor.self, forKey: .vendor)
126	        errorMessage = try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .errorMessage)
127	        severity = try container.decode(OutageSeverity.self, forKey: .severity)
128	        since = try container.decode(ISODate.self, forKey: .since)
129	        if let hrefString = try container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .href) {
130	            guard let parsed = URL(string: hrefString) else {
131	                throw DecodingError.dataCorruptedError(
132	                    forKey: .href, in: container,
133	                    debugDescription: "Invalid URL string: \(hrefString)")
134	            }
135	            href = parsed
136	        } else {
137	            href = nil
138	        }
139	    }
140	}
141	
142	// MARK: - Metric (polymorphic on "type" discriminator)
143	
144	public enum UsageMetric: Codable, Equatable, Sendable {
145	    case timeWindow(name: String, resetAt: ISODate?, windowDuration: DurationMinutes, usagePercent: UsagePercent)
146	    case payAsYouGo(name: String, currentAmount: Double, currency: String)
147	    /// A metric type not yet known to this client; retained for round-trip fidelity.
148	    case unknown(String)
149	
150	    // MARK: Kind
151	
152	    public var kind: MetricKind {
153	        switch self {
154	        case .timeWindow:       .timeWindow
155	        case .payAsYouGo:       .payAsYouGo
156	        case .unknown(let t):   .unknown(t)
157	        }
158	    }
159	
160	    // MARK: Coding
161	
162	    private enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
163	        case type, name, resetAt
164	        case windowDuration = "windowDurationMinutes"
165	        case usagePercent, currentAmount, currency
166	    }
167	
168	    public init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
169	        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
170	        let metricKind = try container.decode(MetricKind.self, forKey: .type)
171	        switch metricKind {
172	        case .timeWindow:
173	            self = .timeWindow(
174	                name: try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .name),
175	                resetAt: try container.decodeIfPresent(ISODate.self, forKey: .resetAt),
176	                windowDuration: try container.decode(DurationMinutes.self, forKey: .windowDuration),
177	                usagePercent: try container.decode(UsagePercent.self, forKey: .usagePercent)
178	            )
179	        case .payAsYouGo:
180	            self = .payAsYouGo(
181	                name: try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .name),
182	                currentAmount: try container.decode(Double.self, forKey: .currentAmount),
183	                currency: try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .currency)
184	            )
185	        case .unknown(let t):
186	            self = .unknown(t)
187	        }
188	    }
189	
190	    public func encode(to encoder: Encoder) throws {
191	        var container = encoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
192	        switch self {
193	        case let .timeWindow(name, resetAt, windowDuration, usagePercent):
194	            try container.encode(MetricKind.timeWindow, forKey: .type)
195	            try container.encode(name, forKey: .name)
196	            try container.encodeIfPresent(resetAt, forKey: .resetAt)
197	            try container.encode(windowDuration, forKey: .windowDuration)
198	            try container.encode(usagePercent, forKey: .usagePercent)
199	        case let .payAsYouGo(name, currentAmount, currency):
200	            try container.encode(MetricKind.payAsYouGo, forKey: .type)
201	            try container.encode(name, forKey: .name)
202	            try container.encode(currentAmount, forKey: .currentAmount)
203	            try container.encode(currency, forKey: .currency)
204	        case let .unknown(t):
205	            // Only the type discriminator round-trips; unknown fields are not preserved
206	            try container.encode(MetricKind.unknown(t), forKey: .type)
207	        }
208	    }
209	}

7	final class AppDelegate: NSObject, NSApplicationDelegate {
8	    private var poller: UsagePoller?
9	    private var accountMonitor: ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor?
10	    private var codexMonitor: CodexActiveAccountMonitor?
11	    private var pidGuard: AppPidGuard?
12	    private var usageStore: UsageStore?
13	    private var refreshState: RefreshState?

18	
19	    /// Shared preferences store — exposed as static so the SwiftUI Settings scene
20	    /// (constructed before applicationDidFinishLaunching) can access it.
21	    static let sharedPreferences: UserDefaultsAppPreferences = UserDefaultsAppPreferences()
22	
23	    /// Shared usage store — populated in applicationDidFinishLaunching. The Settings
24	    /// scene is instantiated before the store exists, so it must read through this
25	    /// mutable holder. Nil until the first launch callback has run.
26	    static var sharedStore: UsageStore?
27	
28	    func applicationDidFinishLaunching(_ notification: Notification) {
29	        NSApplication.shared.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
30	
31	        // No .app bundle ships with this SwiftPM executable, so AppKit has no
32	        // static icon slot — set the Dock / Cmd+Tab icon programmatically.
33	        if let icon = AppIconRenderer.makeImage(pixelSize: 1024) {
34	            NSApplication.shared.applicationIconImage = icon
35	        }
36	
37	        let home = FileManager.default.homeDirectoryForCurrentUser.path
38	        let guard_ = AppPidGuard(cacheDir: "\(home)/.cache/ai-usages-tracker")
39	        do {
40	            try guard_.acquire()
41	        } catch AppPidGuardError.alreadyRunning(let pid, _) {
42	            let alert = NSAlert()
43	            alert.messageText = "AI Usages Tracker is already running"
44	            alert.informativeText = "Another instance is active (PID \(pid)). Use the menu bar icon to quit it before launching a new one."
45	            alert.runModal()
46	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
47	            return
48	        } catch {
49	            let alert = NSAlert()
50	            alert.messageText = "Failed to start AI Usages Tracker"
51	            alert.informativeText = error.localizedDescription
52	            alert.runModal()
53	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
54	            return
55	        }
56	        pidGuard = guard_
57	
58	        Loggers.setPreferences(Self.sharedPreferences)
59	
60	        MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.seedIfNeeded(preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
61	
62	        // Reconcile launch-at-login preference with system state — the user may
63	        // have disabled the entry in System Settings > Login Items directly.
64	        let launchService = LaunchAtLoginService.shared
65	        if Self.sharedPreferences.launchAtLogin != launchService.isEnabled {
66	            Self.sharedPreferences.launchAtLogin = launchService.isEnabled
67	        }
68	
69	        let fileManager = UsagesFileManager.shared
70	        let refreshState = RefreshState()
71	        let codexConnector = CodexConnector()
72	        let poller = UsagePoller(
73	            connectors: [ClaudeCodeConnector(), codexConnector],
74	            statusConnectors: [ClaudeStatusConnector(), CodexStatusConnector()],
75	            fileManager: fileManager,
76	            refreshState: refreshState,
77	            preferences: Self.sharedPreferences
78	        )
79	        let accountMonitor = ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor(
80	            fileManager: fileManager,
81	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak poller] _ in
82	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
83	            }
84	        )
85	        let codexMonitor = CodexActiveAccountMonitor(
86	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak codexConnector, weak poller] _ in
87	                await codexConnector?.invalidateEmailCache()
88	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
89	            }
90	        )
91	        self.poller = poller
92	        self.accountMonitor = accountMonitor
93	        self.codexMonitor = codexMonitor
94	        self.refreshState = refreshState
95	
96	        let usagesPath = fileManager.filePath
97	        let fileWatcher = UsagesFileWatcher(path: usagesPath)
98	        let store = UsageStore(fileWatcher: fileWatcher, preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
99	        self.usageStore = store
100	        Self.sharedStore = store
101	
102	        setupStatusItem(store: store, refreshState: refreshState)
103	        trackStoreChanges(store: store)
104	
105	        Task {
106	            store.start()
107	            await poller.start()
108	            await accountMonitor.start()
109	            await codexMonitor.start()
110	        }
111	    }
112	
113	    func applicationWillTerminate(_ notification: Notification) {
114	        quit()
115	    }
116	
117	    // MARK: - Status item
118	
119	    private func setupStatusItem(store: UsageStore, refreshState: RefreshState) {
120	        let item = NSStatusBar.system.statusItem(withLength: NSStatusItem.variableLength)
121	        statusItem = item
122	
123	        let popover = NSPopover()
124	        popover.behavior = .transient
125	        let hosting = NSHostingController(
126	            rootView: UsageDetailsView(
127	                store: store,
128	                refreshState: refreshState,
129	                onRefresh: { [weak self] in
130	                    await self?.poller?.pollOnce(now: Date(), force: true)
131	                },
132	                onOpenSettings: { [weak self] in
133	                    self?.openSettings()
134	                },
135	                onQuit: { [weak self] in
136	                    self?.quit()
137	                }
138	            )
139	        )
140	        // Let SwiftUI's intrinsic size drive the popover so it grows with content
141	        // up to the screen-ratio cap enforced inside UsageDetailsView.
142	        hosting.sizingOptions = [.preferredContentSize]
143	        popover.contentViewController = hosting
144	        self.popover = popover
145	
146	        if let button = item.button {
147	            button.target = self
148	            button.action = #selector(togglePopover(_:))
149	            // The menu bar appearance can change (wallpaper-driven tinting) without
150	            // the app's effectiveAppearance changing, so we observe the button itself.
151	            appearanceObserver = button.observe(\.effectiveAppearance, options: [.new]) { [weak self] _, _ in
152	                Task { @MainActor in self?.refreshStatusItemImage() }
153	            }
154	        }
155	
156	        refreshStatusItemImage()
157	    }
158	
159	    private func refreshStatusItemImage() {
160	        guard let store = usageStore, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
161	        let isDark = button.effectiveAppearance.bestMatch(from: [.darkAqua, .aqua]) == .darkAqua
162	        let image = MenuBarLabelRenderer.render(
163	            segments: store.menuBarSegments,
164	            fallbackText: store.menuBarText,
165	            isDarkMenuBar: isDark
166	        )
167	        button.image = image
168	    }
169	
170	    @objc private func togglePopover(_ sender: Any?) {
171	        guard let popover, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
172	        if popover.isShown {
173	            popover.performClose(sender)
174	        } else {
175	            popover.show(relativeTo: button.bounds, of: button, preferredEdge: .minY)
176	            popover.contentViewController?.view.window?.makeKey()
177	        }
178	    }
179	
180	    // MARK: - Observation
181	
182	    /// Re-arms `withObservationTracking` after every change so the status item
183	    /// keeps mirroring the store. Tracking fires exactly once per registration.
184	    private func trackStoreChanges(store: UsageStore) {
185	        withObservationTracking {
186	            _ = store.menuBarSegments
187	            _ = store.menuBarText
188	        } onChange: { [weak self] in
189	            Task { @MainActor in
190	                guard let self, let store = self.usageStore else { return }
191	                self.refreshStatusItemImage()
192	                self.trackStoreChanges(store: store)
193	            }
194	        }
195	    }
196	
197	    // MARK: - Settings
198	
199	    private func openSettings() {
200	        popover?.performClose(nil)
201	        // .accessory apps have no dock presence; showSettingsWindow: silently fails
202	        // unless we temporarily switch to .regular so AppKit can make the window key.
203	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
204	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
205	
206	        // SwiftUI creates the Settings window lazily on first showSettingsWindow: —
207	        // if it already exists, reuse it directly to avoid responder-chain races
208	        // that occur during the popover-dismissal + activation-policy transition.
209	        if let settingsWindow = Self.findSettingsWindow() {
210	            settingsWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
211	        } else {
212	            // First-time path: AppKit needs a runloop tick after the .accessory →
213	            // .regular transition to install the standard App menu (which is where
214	            // SwiftUI wires the Settings… action). sendAction with a nil target
215	            // relies on that wiring, so we trigger the menu item directly once
216	            // AppKit has had a chance to build it.
217	            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.15) {
218	                Self.triggerSettingsMenuItem()
219	                if let settingsWindow = Self.findSettingsWindow() {
220	                    settingsWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
221	                }
222	            }
223	        }
224	
225	        // Restore .accessory policy once the settings window is closed.
226	        if settingsWindowObserver == nil {
227	            settingsWindowObserver = NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
228	                forName: NSWindow.willCloseNotification,
229	                object: nil,
230	                queue: .main
231	            ) { [weak self] notification in
232	                guard let self else { return }
233	                // Only react to the Settings window closing — popovers and other
234	                // transient windows also emit willClose.
235	                guard let closingWindow = notification.object as? NSWindow else { return }
236	                let windowIdentifier = closingWindow.identifier?.rawValue ?? ""
237	                guard windowIdentifier.contains("Settings") || windowIdentifier.contains("Preferences") else { return }
238	                // willClose fires while the window is still visible and before
239	                // AppKit tears it down; defer to the next runloop tick so the
240	                // activation-policy change isn't overridden by pending AppKit work.
241	                DispatchQueue.main.async {
242	                    NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
243	                    // Accessory transition doesn't always remove the app from the
244	                    // Cmd+Tab / app-switcher list until another app is activated.
245	                    if let frontmost = NSWorkspace.shared.runningApplications.first(where: {
246	                        $0.isActive && $0.processIdentifier != ProcessInfo.processInfo.processIdentifier
247	                    }) {
248	                        frontmost.activate()
249	                    } else {
250	                        NSApp.hide(nil)
251	                    }
252	                    if let token = self.settingsWindowObserver {
253	                        NotificationCenter.default.removeObserver(token)
254	                        self.settingsWindowObserver = nil
255	                    }
256	                }
257	            }
258	        }
259	    }
260	
261	    /// SwiftUI tags its Settings window with an identifier containing "Settings"
262	    /// (or "Preferences" on older SDKs). Match on the stable identifier rather
263	    /// than localized titles.
264	    private static func findSettingsWindow() -> NSWindow? {
265	        NSApp.windows.first { window in
266	            let identifier = window.identifier?.rawValue ?? ""
267	            return identifier.contains("Settings") || identifier.contains("Preferences")
268	        }
269	    }
270	
271	    /// Walk the main menu looking for the App menu's Settings… (or Preferences…)
272	    /// item and invoke it. Going through the menu guarantees the action is
273	    /// routed to whichever responder SwiftUI wired up, instead of relying on
274	    /// `sendAction(_:to:from:)` to resolve a nil target in the responder chain
275	    /// — which is unreliable right after an activation-policy switch.
276	    private static func triggerSettingsMenuItem() {
277	        guard let mainMenu = NSApp.mainMenu else { return }
278	        for topItem in mainMenu.items {
279	            guard let submenu = topItem.submenu else { continue }
280	            for (index, item) in submenu.items.enumerated() {
281	                let title = item.title
282	                if title.hasPrefix("Settings") || title.hasPrefix("Preferences") {
283	                    submenu.performActionForItem(at: index)
284	                    return
285	                }
286	            }
287	        }
288	    }
289	
290	    // MARK: - Quit
291	
292	    private func quit() {
293	        let pollerRef = poller
294	        let monitorRef = accountMonitor
295	        let codexMonitorRef = codexMonitor
296	        Task {
297	            await pollerRef?.stop()
298	            await monitorRef?.stop()
299	            await codexMonitorRef?.stop()
300	        }
301	        usageStore?.stop()
302	        pidGuard?.release()
303	        NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
304	    }
305	}

# SwiftLint configuration for AIUsagesTrackers.
#
# Rule codes mirror .claude/rules/swift-quality.md — keep the mapping in sync.
# Severities: `error` fails the build; `warning` surfaces in logs / IDE.

included:
  - Sources
  - Tests

# Narrow the SwiftLint surface to exactly the codes documented in
# .claude/rules/swift-quality.md. Built-in rules are deliberately NOT enabled —
# we want a low-noise, high-signal lint focused on the patterns that reviews
# keep flagging. Reach for built-in rules only if a review finding recurs that
# a custom rule cannot express precisely.
only_rules:
  - custom_rules

custom_rules:
  e1_try_silencing_critical_fs:
    name: "try? on critical filesystem op"
    regex: 'try\?\s+(FileManager\.default\.)?(createDirectory|moveItem|copyItem|setAttributes)\b'
    message: "try? swallows failure on a correctness-critical FS op — use do/catch (SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING §1)"
    severity: error

  e2_flock_lock_ex_without_nb:
    name: "flock LOCK_EX without LOCK_NB"
    regex: 'flock\([^)]*LOCK_EX(?![^)]*LOCK_NB)'
    message: "flock with LOCK_EX must be OR'd with LOCK_NB and wrapped in a timeout loop (SWIFT-CONCURRENCY / SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS)"
    severity: error
    excluded:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/.*\\.swift"

  w1_nonisolated_unsafe:
    name: "nonisolated(unsafe) in production"
    regex: 'nonisolated\(unsafe\)'
    excluded_match_kinds:
      - comment
      - doccomment
    message: "nonisolated(unsafe) bypasses Swift isolation — prefer actor-isolated storage or per-call allocation (SWIFT-CONCURRENCY)"
    severity: warning
    excluded:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/.*\\.swift"

  w3_task_sleep_literal_in_tests:
    name: "Task.sleep with fixed literal in tests"
    regex: 'Task\.sleep\([^)]*[0-9]'
    message: "Task.sleep with a fixed literal is fragile under CI load — poll the observable state with the eventually() helper (SWIFT-TESTABILITY)"
    severity: warning
    included:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/.*\\.swift"

  w4_unchecked_sendable:
    name: "@unchecked Sendable"
    regex: '@unchecked\s+Sendable'
    excluded_match_kinds:
      - comment
      - doccomment
    message: "@unchecked Sendable requires an adjacent justification comment describing the single-threaded contract (SWIFT-CONCURRENCY)"
    severity: warning

  w5_duration_literal_in_comment:
    name: "Duration literal embedded in comment"
    regex: '\b\d+\s*(hours?|days?|minutes?|seconds?|ms)\b'
    match_kinds:
      - comment
    message: "Comment embeds a bare `N unit` duration — lift to a named constant whose declaration site carries the rationale (SWIFT-TESTABILITY §Comment WHY)"
    severity: warning

**Changements appliqués** :

1. **Format JSONL** : une ligne = un `TickSnapshot` complet avec `timestamp` en premier, puis un tableau `accounts` regroupant vendor + account + toutes leurs `metrics`. Nouveaux types dans `Models/TickSnapshot.swift` : `TickSnapshot`, `AccountSnapshot`, `MetricSnapshot`.

2. **Dédup par hash SHA256** (via CryptoKit) : `SnapshotRecorder.hash(of:)` encode le tableau `accounts` en JSON avec `sortedKeys` (canonicalisation) puis hashe. Le `lastWrittenHash` remplace l'ancienne signature. Le hash est intentionnellement calculé sans le timestamp.

3. **Nouveaux tests** vérifiant explicitement les règles :
   - `writes a single line per tick containing every vendor/account/metric`
   - `serialized line starts with the timestamp field for readability` (vérifie que le JSON commence par `{"timestamp":`)
   - `skips a tick whose payload hash matches the previous write`
   - `writes again when any metric value changes`
   - `writes again when an account appears or disappears`
   - `hash ignores the timestamp so dedup only compares payloads`

content.text:
361 tests OK.

**Bug reproduit et fixé** :

Le hash de dédup vivait uniquement en mémoire — au restart de l'app, le 1er tick écrivait une ligne identique à la dernière sur disque.

**Fix** : `SnapshotRecorder` lit maintenant la dernière ligne du fichier du jour au premier tick après init pour seeder `lastWrittenHash` (méthode `lastHashOnDisk(for:)` + extracteur `lastLineData(in:)`). Le seed se fait une seule fois (flag `didSeedHashFromDisk`) et reste cantonné au fichier du jour : un restart un nouveau jour démarre proprement le nouveau fichier.

**Tests ajoutés** :
- `dedup persists across recorder restarts when payload is identical` — reproduit le bug. Échoue sans le fix, passe avec.
- `a restart on a brand-new day starts the new file with a single line` — garantit que le seed disque ne lit pas le fichier de la veille.

**Bug bonus découvert** : ma première implémentation s'appuyait sur l'ordre des `CodingKeys` pour avoir `timestamp` en premier dans le JSON, mais `JSONEncoder` réordonne (alphabétiquement). Le test `serialized line starts with the timestamp field for readability` l'a attrapé en CI alors qu'il passait isolément. Fix : encodage manuel du top-level object pour garantir l'ordre.

Total : 18 tests Snapshot (+ 343 existants) = 361 verts.

content.text:
# Smart commit

Tu dois committer les changements git en cours dans le repo courant en suivant les guidelines ci-dessous.

## Contexte utilisateur

Intent fourni par l'utilisateur (peut être vide) : ``

## Étapes

### 1. Analyser l'état du repo

Lance en parallèle :
- `git status` (sans `-uall`)
- `git diff --staged` et `git diff` pour voir les changements
- `git log -n 5 --pretty=format:'%h %s%n%b%n---'` pour voir les 4-5 derniers commits

### 2. Détecter les conventions du projet

À partir des 4-5 derniers commits :
- **Conventional commits ?** Si la majorité suit le format `type(scope): message` (feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf, build, ci...) → utilise ce format. Sinon → format libre.
- **Langue ?** Détecte la langue dominante des messages (français, anglais, autre) → utilise la même langue. En cas de doute, français.
- **Style ?** Impératif vs descriptif, majuscule initiale ou non, présence de scope, etc. → imite.

### 3. Détecter un éventuel fixup

Si l'intent utilisateur mentionne un correctif (mots-clés : "fix", "corrige", "répare", "bug", "issue", "problème", "régression", "broken"...) :

1. Identifie les fichiers modifiés (`git diff --name-only HEAD` + fichiers staged).
2. Pour chaque fichier, regarde `git log -n 10 --oneline -- <fichier>` pour voir quels commits récents l'ont touché.
3. Si **un seul commit récent** (dans les ~5 derniers, non encore pushé sur main/master partagé — vérifie avec `git log @{u}..HEAD` si upstream) est clairement à l'origine du code cassé, propose un **`git commit --fixup=<sha>`** au lieu d'un commit classique.
4. Si ambigu ou si le commit date d'avant `@{u}` (déjà pushé en amont partagé), reste sur un commit classique.

Ne fais **pas** de rebase autosquash automatiquement — laisse ce soin à l'utilisateur.

### 4. Rédiger le message

Règles impératives :
- **Le message explique le POURQUOI**, pas le QUOI. Le diff dit déjà ce qui change. Le message dit pourquoi ce changement existe (motivation, contrainte, problème résolu, contexte métier).
- Sujet court (≤72 caractères), en suivant la convention détectée.
- Si le pourquoi ne tient pas dans le sujet, ajoute un corps (ligne vide + explication). Sinon, pas de corps inutile.
- Pas de name-dropping de fichiers/fonctions dans le sujet — le diff suffit.
- Pas de phrases vides genre « Mise à jour du code » ou « Divers fixes ».

Si l'intent utilisateur est vide ou trop vague pour déduire le pourquoi, et que le diff ne le rend pas évident, **demande à l'utilisateur** avant de committer plutôt que d'inventer.

### 5. Staging et commit

- Si rien n'est staged, stage les fichiers pertinents **par nom** (jamais `git add -A` ni `git add .` pour éviter d'embarquer secrets/binaires).
- Ignore les fichiers suspects (`.env`, `credentials*`, clés privées, dumps volumineux). Préviens l'utilisateur si tu en vois.
- Crée le commit via HEREDOC pour préserver le formatage multiligne.
- **Ne jamais** utiliser `--no-verify`, `--amend` (sauf demande explicite), ni `--no-gpg-sign`.
- Si un hook pre-commit échoue : diagnostique la cause, corrige, re-stage, et crée un **nouveau** commit (pas d'amend).

### 6. Vérification finale

Après commit, lance `git status` et `git log -1` pour confirmer. Reporte brièvement : sha court + sujet + (fixup le cas échéant).

## Garde-fous

- Ne push jamais.
- Ne crée pas de commit vide.
- Ne touche pas à `git config`.
- Si plusieurs "unités logiques" distinctes sont mélangées dans le diff, préviens l'utilisateur et propose de splitter plutôt que de tout entasser. Tu peux utiliser `git stash` (avec `-u`/`--keep-index` si pertinent) pour isoler des sous-ensembles — mais cette commande n'est **pas** auto-approuvée, l'utilisateur devra confirmer à chaque usage : ne l'utilise que si c'est vraiment nécessaire.

- Ajout de l'enregistrement periodique (1x par minute) des snapshots d'usage
- Format JSONL (une ligne par tick avec tous les accounts/métriques)
- Dédup par hash du contenu pour éviter les doublons sur disque
- Séparation du scheduler de UsagePoller (60s fixe vs interval utilisateur)
- Persistance de la dédup entre restarts en relisant la dernière ligne du fichier

```
feat(snapshots): add periodic usage history recording with content-based deduplication

```

La dédup fonctionne via hash SHA256 du payload (timestamp exclu), donc les ticks identiques consécutifs n'écrivent qu'une seule ligne. Ce hash persiste au redémarrage en lisant la dernière ligne du fichier quotidien, évitant les doublons après un crash. Le timestamp s'écrit en premier pour la lisibilité, et les comptes sans métriques connues sont filtrés. 18 tests couvrent les cas nominaux (append, skip vide, skip inchangé), création de fichier, append post-restart, changement de jour, validité JSON et gestion des métriques inconnues.
